name: Kai's Spinach Pie
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: "Shanks, Mira L."
Remove stems from spinach and wash. Cut onions and saute in water until tender. Wash and slice mushrooms and add to the onions (cook until all water evaporates). Remove onions & mushrooms from heat and put into a large bowl. Saute spinach in water tossing frequently until all liquid evaporates. Mix onions/mushrooms and spinach together in bowl, ensuring onions and mushrooms are evenly distributed throughout the spinach. Beat egg whites lightly. Add grated cheese to egg mixture and mix thoroughly. Add spinach mixture to egg and cheese mixture and stir until mixed well.
CRUST: The dough is paper thin and requires many layers. Use a rectangular glass baking dish, sprayed lightly with Pam. Quickly layer 5-7 sheets on the bottom of the dish, spraying each layer lightly with Pam. Put half of your spinach mixture on top. Place another 5-7 sheets of Phyllo. Then place the remaining of your spinach mixture. Top with 10 to 15 sheets of phyllo, spraying lightly with Pam between each layer. Remove any excess dough around the edges. Moisten fingertips and seal around the edges like a normal pie crust. Spray top lightly with Pam. Using a sharp knife, cut through the top layer in the pattern desired (e.g., rectangular pattern for pieces or diamond shapes as in Baklava). Bake at 400-425°F oven for 30 minutes.
Use healthy choice fat-free grated cheeses made from skim milk. Their cheese tastes and melts like high-fat cheeses in this recipe. This dish is really good!
